cases.title: Status history retrieval makes registration transitions traceable per testing-plan §3
preconditions: RegistrationOpen event with available capacity
Participant registered then cancelled own registration
OrganizerAdmin actor with event scope
steps: Invoke registrationService.register for participant on event
Invoke registrationService.cancel for the registration
Call auditService.listStatusHistory(eventId, { registrationId, sort: "createdAt:asc" })
cases.expected: At least two entries: registration.accepted with afterState Registered and registration.cancelled with afterState CancelledByUser; each entry includes registrationId, occurredAt, and before/after state; satisfies testing-plan §3 row: Status history retrieval → transitions traceable (AC-12)

cases.title: Registration module writes append-only status history rows via audit module boundary
preconditions: RegistrationOpen event with available capacity
Real Postgres test database with transaction rollback per test
Participant registered and subsequently cancelled
steps: Invoke registrationService.register then registrationService.cancel within registration module
Query audit_logs or status_history store for eventId and registrationId
Verify registration module invoked audit module within transactional boundary
cases.expected: Append-only history rows persisted with registration.accepted and registration.cancelled actions, before/after states, actorId, and occurredAt; module-integration boundary per audit module contract

cases.title: Flow C GET status-history over HTTP traces register-to-cancel transitions
preconditions: E2E RegistrationOpen event with capacity 5
Participant registered then cancelled via POST /registrations/{id}/cancel
OrganizerAdmin JWT with event scope
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history?registrationId={registrationId}&sort=createdAt:asc with organizer Authorization bearer token
Locate registration.accepted and registration.cancelled items in paginated envelope
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with items array containing registration.accepted (afterState Registered) and registration.cancelled (afterState CancelledByUser) for the same registrationId with occurredAt timestamps; satisfies Flow C governance traceability alongside AC-11

cases.title: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history returns HTTP 200 paginated envelope
preconditions: Event with at least one registration status change recorded
OrganizerAdmin JWT with event scope
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history?page=1&pageSize=20&sort=createdAt:desc with admin Authorization bearer token
Verify HTTP status code and response envelope shape
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with items array, page, pageSize, total, totalPages; at least one item has action registration.accepted with afterState visible per API design §2.5 and §3

cases.title: Organizer registrations page opens status history dialog with transition columns
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin logged in with access to event that has registrations with status history
Organizer event registrations page reachable at /organizer/events/{eventId}/registrations
steps: Navigate to organizer event registrations page
Click Status history button on a registration row
Observe dialog table columns for When, Transition (beforeState → afterState), Actor, and Action
Use pagination controls inside dialog when history exceeds pageSize
cases.expected: Dialog renders registration status history with beforeState → afterState transition, actor identifier, and occurredAt timestamp; server-driven pagination inside dialog does not render unbounded dataset client-side

cases.title: OrganizerAdmin lists event-wide status history with paginated envelope and default sort
preconditions: Event with multiple registration status changes across participants
Authenticated OrganizerAdmin actor
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history?page=1&pageSize=20 with admin token
Verify response envelope fields and default createdAt:desc ordering
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with items, page, pageSize, total, totalPages; entries sorted by createdAt descending by default per API design §3

cases.title: registrationId filter returns only that registration's transitions from repository
preconditions: Event with two distinct participant registrations each having status history
OrganizerAdmin actor
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history?registrationId={registrationIdA}
Verify all returned items belong to registrationIdA
cases.expected: Every item in response has registrationId matching filter; no entries from other registrations leak into filtered result

cases.title: Waitlisted to Registered promotion is traceable in status history
preconditions: Full event with waitlist enabled
Participant waitlisted then promoted after cancellation frees a seat
steps: Register participant when event full to receive Waitlisted state
Cancel an existing Registered registration to trigger FIFO promotion
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history?registrationId={promotedRegistrationId}&sort=createdAt:asc with organizer token
cases.expected: History includes waitlist entry and subsequent promotion transition with afterState Registered; promotion from Waitlisted traceable per Flow B waitlist lifecycle

cases.title: Check-in and event completion produce CheckedIn and Attended history entries
preconditions: Registered participant with valid in-window check-in recorded
Event progressed to Completed triggering attendance finalization
steps: Perform valid check-in for registration
Complete event to finalize attendance
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history?registrationId={registrationId}&sort=createdAt:asc
cases.expected: History includes checkin.recorded with afterState reflecting check-in and transition to Attended after event completion; timestamps and states support operational traceability

cases.title: OrganizerStaff on assigned event can read status history
preconditions: Event with at least one registration status change
OrganizerStaff JWT assigned to the event
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history?page=1&pageSize=10 with staff Authorization bearer token
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with paginated envelope; items include registration.accepted; staff read access permitted for operational lists per FR-30 while audit-logs remain admin-only

cases.title: Participant cannot read event status history
preconditions: RegistrationOpen event with status history entries
Participant JWT registered on the event
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history with participant Authorization bearer token
cases.expected: HTTP 403 forbidden; registration status history restricted to organizer roles per permission matrix

cases.title: Unauthenticated GET status-history returns 401
preconditions: Event with existing status history entries
No Authorization header on request
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history without bearer token
cases.expected: HTTP 401 unauthenticated; status history data not exposed

cases.title: Invalid pagination on status-history returns INVALID_PAGINATION
preconditions: Event with status history entries
OrganizerAdmin JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history?page=0
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history?page=1&pageSize=101
cases.expected: HTTP 400 with INVALID_PAGINATION for page less than 1 or pageSize above max 100; no partial history data returned

cases.title: Status history page beyond totalPages returns empty items with valid metadata
preconditions: Event with known status history entry count
OrganizerAdmin actor
steps: Call listStatusHistory with page=1 pageSize=2 to read total and totalPages
Call listStatusHistory with page exceeding totalPages
cases.expected: Beyond-last-page response has items: [] with matching total and totalPages per pagination contract

cases.title: registrationId filter for registration with no history returns empty paginated result
preconditions: Valid registrationId UUID that has never transitioned on the event
OrganizerAdmin actor
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/status-history?registrationId={unusedRegistrationId}
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with items: [], total 0, totalPages 0 or 1 per envelope rules; no error for empty history

cases.title: OrganizerStaff cannot read status history for out-of-scope event
preconditions: Event with status history entries not assigned to staff actor
OrganizerStaff JWT scoped to a different event
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{unassignedEventId}/status-history with staff Authorization bearer token
cases.expected: HTTP 403 or 404 per scope-hiding policy; status history not exposed outside assigned event scope

cases.title: Sequential registration transitions produce distinct chronologically ordered history entries
preconditions: RegistrationOpen event
Single registration progressing through register, check-in, and event completion
steps: Register participant
Check in within valid window
Complete event
List status history with sort createdAt:asc for the registrationId
cases.expected: Multiple distinct entries with monotonic occurredAt ordering; each transition (Registered, CheckedIn, Attended) captured as separate immutable records

cases.title: Status history entries include actorId, actorRole, and occurredAt for audit correlation
preconditions: Registration status change recorded by known Participant actor
steps: Retrieve status history entry for registration.accepted action
Inspect actorId, actorRole, and occurredAt fields
cases.expected: Entry includes non-null actorId matching initiating participant, actorRole Participant, and ISO occurredAt timestamp; supports error and audit log correlation per NFR-10

cases.title: Status history records are immutable with no mutation API
preconditions: Existing status history entry persisted for an event
Audit module guardrail: never allow audit record mutation
steps: Confirm no PATCH/PUT/DELETE endpoint exists for status-history items
Re-query same history entry after subsequent unrelated registrations
cases.expected: Original entry unchanged; no client-facing mutation path; append-only history preserved per audit module invariant

cases.title: Status history list returns accurate total count across multiple registrations
preconditions: Event with three or more participant registrations each producing at least one history entry
steps: Call listStatusHistory without registrationId filter with pageSize=2
Compare total field against known transition count
cases.expected: total reflects all registration status transitions for the event; pageSize slices items without losing count accuracy per FR-31 pagination contract
